title: cassine oligantha
intro: Cassine oligantha is a plant species in the Anacardiaceae family, native to the Caribbean region, particularly in the island of Martinique. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Cassine oligantha has been traditionally used to alleviate health issues such as bronchitis, cough, and skin conditions, making it a valuable herbal remedy in the region. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical ointments, allowing for various methods of consumption and application.
